briefing.opening: Hello everyone. I will now begin the climate briefing for July 15, 2026.

sections.title: 🔴 Deepening Global Climate Crisis and Impacts
sections.content: News collected today once again warns of the severity of the deepening global climate crisis. As described by the phrase 'boiling planet' [1, 4], record-breaking heatwaves are occurring in Colorado and Europe, exacerbating climate change's 'compounding extreme events' [47, 61, 65, 71, 72]. This climate change is increasing the risk of spreading diseases such as cyclosporiasis [42] and dengue fever [48], and is having fatal impacts on ecosystems, including changes in amphibian diets [46] and an 89% decline in the British curlew population [63]. Furthermore, research indicates that climate change could double household water bills in some cities in the future [55], and that plant disease resistance rules may be reshaped, posing a threat to food security [54]. The situation is extremely urgent, to the extent that climate change is threatening human mental health [50] and, ultimately, has prompted commentary that we are 'baking to death' [51].

sections.title: ⚠️ Domestic Climate Crisis Warnings and Response Challenges
sections.content: Domestically, warnings about the climate crisis are being issued, emphasizing the importance of practical responses. Warnings have been raised that climate change could lead to the 'worst high-temperature crisis ever' [6], and the South Chungcheong Provincial Council urged an upgrade of river maintenance standards and a complete overhaul of drainage infrastructure to respond to the climate crisis [10]. Notably, news that Naver and Kakao's greenhouse gas emissions have increased by 80% in the past two years raises awareness about the carbon footprint of the digital industry [25]. More than half of Seoul's heat shelters were not properly operated despite heatwave advisories, raising concerns for citizens' safety [38], and four years after the introduction of the Climate Change Impact Assessment system, 'implementation management' rather than 'assessment' is being pointed out as a greater challenge [3].

sections.title: 🌱 Carbon Neutrality Practices and Renewable Energy Transition Efforts
sections.content: Amidst these crises, positive domestic and international efforts to address climate change continue. Domestically, carbon neutrality practice campaigns are being carried out by various entities [18, 27, 30], starting with Sejong City's eco-friendly carbon neutrality education for elementary school students [11], followed by Nambu Power's energy saving campaign [12], and Kangwon Land and Sangji University's carbon neutrality promotion activities [13]. Jangseong-gun and Buyeo-gun are encouraging citizen participation through local currency payments and incentives for carbon neutrality practices [20, 21, 22, 23, 26, 28], and Korea Midland Power aims to enhance renewable energy grid stability by establishing AI-based ESS [32]. Internationally, the European Investment Bank (EIB) is strengthening climate change response capabilities by supporting the modernization of drinking water infrastructure in the North Holland region of the Netherlands [2], and Jeff Bezos has pledged the largest personal donation ever to climate change philanthropy [41], demonstrating continued investment and efforts for climate change mitigation.

sections.title: 📰 Policy and Technological Discussions for Climate Change Response
sections.content: Policy directions and technological discussions for responding to climate change are also actively underway. Along with strengthened greenhouse gas standards for automobiles [5], Gyeongsangbuk-do is reshaping the steel industry to align with the carbon-neutral era [15], and the carbon neutrality report card for the manufacturing sector is being reviewed following its 2050 net-zero target [16]. The economic growth strategy for the second half of 2026 included a target of 100GW for renewable energy supply [35], but concerns have been raised that renewable energy still has a long way to go in an era of surging electricity demand [31], and that public opinion favoring nuclear power is shaking the identity of renewable energy transition policies [37]. Meanwhile, various approaches are being explored, such as reimagining citizen learning in the face of global climate change [52] and discussing the utilization of digital twins as a tool for environmental and climate change management [53].

briefing.closing: Today's briefing highlighted the severity of the global climate crisis, various domestic and international response efforts, and the challenges that still remain. Our collective and sustained attention and action are essential in confronting the threats of climate change. This concludes today's climate briefing.

articles.title: [Exclusive] Seoul City Announced 24-Hour Opening of District Office Cooling Centers···Over Half 'Unavailable'

articles.description: The entrance to Seoul's A District Office was firmly closed on the morning of the 14th, despite a heatwave advisory being in effect. Reporter Kim Tae-wook. Seoul City announced that it would open 'cooling centers' established in district office buildings 24 hours a day as a heatwave countermeasure, but it was found that less than half of them were operating properly. Seoul City stated that it would re-inspect the operational status of the district offices. Following the heatwave advisory issued across Seoul on the 11th, southeastern and western parts of the city...

summary.phenomenon: Following the heatwave advisory issued on the 11th, more than half of Seoul's cooling centers, which were announced to be open 24 hours, were not operating properly, including the A District Office's cooling center remaining closed on the morning of the 14th.
summary.cause: Cooling centers were needed for citizens to escape the heat due to the heatwave advisory, but operational deficiencies occurred.
summary.outlook: Seoul City plans to re-inspect the operational status of district offices and attempt to implement improvements.

articles.original_title: ‘Unprecedented’ changes in UK climate are normalising extremes, report says
articles.description: Annual State of the UK Climate analysis finds last four years in UK are in top five hottest on recordThe UK’s climatic extremes are becoming increasingly normal, a report has found, with last year the hottest on record and further “unprecedented changes” likely to break the record again soon.Data stretching back to 1884 shows the UK has never experienced a year as hot as 2025, according to the annual State of the UK Climate report, with temperatures pushed to dizzying heights by carbon pollution

articles.title: 앤디 번햄은 기후 문제에 신속히 행동해야 한다 – 그렇지 않으면 '탈선' 파국적 악순환에 갇힐 위험이 있다 | 로리 레이번
articles.original_title: Andy Burnham must act fast on the climate – or risk getting stuck in a ‘derailment’ doom loop | Laurie Laybourn
articles.description: Around the world, climate-sceptic parties are exploiting floods and fires to make political capital. Without urgent changes, this deadly spiral will continueRecent unprecedented heatwaves in the UK may have killed thousands of people. Children are suffering in overheating schools. NHS trusts are straining under record-breaking demand. This all comes after climate extremes have even affected national security, with three of Britain’s five worst harvests coming since 2020, impairing food security.

summary.phenomenon: The UK has recently experienced unprecedented heatwaves, which may have led to thousands of deaths, overheating schools, and record-breaking demand on the NHS (National Health Service). Since 2020, three of Britain's five worst harvests have occurred, impacting food security.
summary.cause: These phenomena are caused by 'climate extremes,' which climate-sceptic parties are exploiting for political gain.
summary.outlook: Without urgent changes, the 'deadly spiral' of climate extremes and their political exploitation will continue, risking entrapment in a 'catastrophic runaway spiral'.

articles.original_description: Around the world, climate-sceptic parties are exploiting floods and fires to make political capital. Without urgent changes, this deadly spiral will continueRecent unprecedented heatwaves in the UK may have killed thousands of people. Children are suffering in overheating schools. NHS trusts are straining under record-breaking demand. This all comes after climate extremes have even affected national security, with three of Britain’s five worst harvests coming since 2020, impairing food security.

articles.title: Kemi declares war on net zero – and what remains of her party
articles.description: Badenoch’s ideological purge gathers pace as moderates are cast out and the Tories edge ever closer to political extinctionYou have to hand it to Kemi Badenoch. She doesn’t take any prisoners. While the rest of us have been trying to survive a heatwave and are wondering if air-con may be needed for future summers, the Tory leader has taken a more hardline approach to climate change. Call it Schrödinger’s climate change. It’s both happening and not happening at the same time.Kemi is just about sa
